topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n Structured data for component rendering\n    summary: str  # 2-3 sentence narration for audio mode\n    urgency: Urgency\n    coverage: Optional[dict] = None  # Which fetch sources succeeded\n    caveats: Optional[list[str]] = None  # Caveats from fetch failures\n\n\nclass SynthesizeStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Synthesize strand: LLM-powered result synthesis.\n\n    Takes fetched context and produces structured results with:\n    - Component-ready data\n    - Conversational summary\n    - Urgency classification\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load synthesize prompt from disk (hot-reload).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load synthesize prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"You are a helpful assistant that synthesizes data into structured results.\"\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load urgency classification rules from prompts/urgency.md (hot-reload).\n\n        urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py. Returns \"\" on failure so synthesis still\n        functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    async def synthesize(self, request: SynthesizeRequest) -> SynthesizeResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Synthesize fetched context into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            request: SynthesizeRequest with fetched context\n\n        Returns:\n            SynthesizeResult with data, summary, and urgency\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        prompt = self._load_prompt()\n\n        # Splice urgency.md rules into the system prompt (hot-reloadable).\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = f\"{prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n        else:\n            system_prompt = prompt\n\n        # Build user message with intent spec and fetched context\n        user_message = self._build_user_message(request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Synthesizing intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # Use Haiku for faster synthesis\n                max_tokens=1024,  # Reduced from 4096 - most outputs are smaller\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for more deterministic, faster outputs\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences and parse JSON (using centralized parser for optimal performance)\n            raw = strip_markdown_fences(response)\n            result_data = json.loads(raw)\n\n            # Extract fields\n            data = result_data.get(\"data\", {})\n            summary = result_data.get(\"summary\", \"\")\n            urgency_str = result_data.get(\"urgency\", request.urgency)\n\n            # Map urgency string to enum\n            try:\n                urgency = Urgency(urgency_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                urgency = Urgency.NORMAL\n\n            # Build coverage and caveats from fetch result\n            coverage = None\n            caveats = None\n            if request.fetched_context:\n                coverage = {\n                    \"total_sources\": request.fetched_context.coverage.total_sources,\n                    \"succeeded\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.succeeded),\n                    \"timed_out\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.timed_out),\n                    \"failed\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.failed),\n                }\n                caveats = request.fetched_context.caveats\n\n            result = SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=urgency,\n                coverage=coverage,\n                caveats=caveats,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Synthesis complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(data)} data fields, urgency={urgency.value}\"\n            )\n\n            return result\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse synthesize response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return minimal result\n            return SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data={\"type\": \"error\", \"error\": \"Failed to parse synthesis response\"},\n                summary=\"An error occurred while processing the result.\",\n                urgency=Urgency.NORMAL,\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Synthesis failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    def _build_user_message(self, request: SynthesizeRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build user message for LLM call.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            f\"## Intent Specification\",\n            f\"Intent Type: {request.intent_type.value}\",\n            f\"Project: {request.project_slug or 'none'}\",\n            f\"Urgency: {request.urgency}\",\n            f\"\",\n            f\"## Utterance\",\n            f\"{request.utterance}\",\n            f\"\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.fetched_context:\n            lines.append(\"## Fetched Context\")\n\n            # Add results from each source\n            for source, source_result in request.fetched_context.sources.items():\n                lines.append(f\"\\n### {source.value}\")\n                if source_result.status == \"success\":\n                    # Pretty-print the data\n                    lines.append(f\"```json\")\n                    lines.append(json.dumps(source_result.data, indent=2))\n                    lines.append(f\"```\")\n                else:\n                    lines.append(f\"Status: {source_result.status}\")\n                    if source_result.error:\n                        lines.append(f\"Error: {source_result.error}\")\n\n            # Add coverage info\n            if request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                lines.append(f\"\\n## Caveats\")\n                for caveat in request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                    lines.append(f\"- {caveat}\")\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\nPlease synthesize this into a structured result.\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# Global synthesize strand instance\n_synthesize_strand: Optional[SynthesizeStrand] = None\n\n\ndef get_synthesize_strand(prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> SynthesizeStrand:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global synthesize strand instance.\"\"\"\n    global _synthesize_strand\n    if _synthesize_strand is None:\n        _synthesize_strand = SynthesizeStrand(prompt_path=prompt_path)\n    return _synthesize_strand\n\n\nasync def synthesize_intent(request: SynthesizeRequest) -> SynthesizeResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to synthesize an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: SynthesizeRequest\n\n    Returns:\n        SynthesizeResult\n    \"\"\"\n    strand = get_synthesize_strand()\n    return await strand.synthesize(request)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":141,"oldLines":9,"newStart":141,"newLines":16,"lines":["                 temperature=0.3,  # Returns \"\" on failure so synthesis still\n        functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    async def synthesize(self, request: SynthesizeRequest) -> SynthesizeResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Synthesize fetched context into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            request: SynthesizeRequest with fetched context\n\n        Returns:\n            SynthesizeResult with data, summary, and urgency\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        prompt = self._load_prompt()\n\n        # Splice urgency.md rules into the system prompt (hot-reloadable).\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = f\"{prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n        else:\n            system_prompt = prompt\n\n        # Build user message with intent spec and fetched context\n        user_message = self._build_user_message(request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Synthesizing intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # Use Haiku for faster synthesis\n                max_tokens=1024,  # Reduced from 4096 - most outputs are smaller\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for more deterministic, faster outputs\n            )\n\n            # Parse JSON response using centralized parser\n            # Note: Synthesize uses fallback result pattern (not corrective retry)\n            # because fetch operations are expensive and should not be discarded.\n            # See docs/error-handling-standardization.md for pattern comparison.\n            try:\n                result_data = parse_llm_response(response)\n            except ParseLLMError as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Failed to parse synthesize response: {e}\")\n                # Fall through to fallback result below\n                raise json.JSONDecodeError(str(e), doc=\"\", pos=0) from e\n\n            # Extract fields\n            data = result_data.get(\"data\", {})\n            summary = result_data.get(\"summary\", \"\")\n            urgency_str = result_data.get(\"urgency\", request.urgency)\n\n            # Map urgency string to enum\n            try:\n                urgency = Urgency(urgency_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                urgency = Urgency.NORMAL\n\n            # Build coverage and caveats from fetch result\n            coverage = None\n            caveats = None\n            if request.fetched_context:\n                coverage = {\n                    \"total_sources\": request.fetched_context.coverage.total_sources,\n                    \"succeeded\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.succeeded),\n                    \"timed_out\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.timed_out),\n                    \"failed\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.failed),\n                }\n                caveats = request.fetched_context.caveats\n\n            result = SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=urgency,\n                coverage=coverage,\n                caveats=caveats,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Synthesis complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(data)} data fields, urgency={urgency.value}\"\n            )\n\n            return result\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse synthesize response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return minimal result\n            return SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data={\"type\": \"error\", \"error\": \"Failed to parse synthesis response\"},\n                summary=\"An error occurred while processing the result.\",\n                urgency=Urgency.NORMAL,\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Synthesis failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    def _build_user_message(self, request: SynthesizeRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build user message for LLM call.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            f\"## Intent Specification\",\n            f\"Intent Type: {request.intent_type.value}\",\n            f\"Project: {request.project_slug or 'none'}\",\n            f\"Urgency: {request.urgency}\",\n            f\"\",\n            f\"## Utterance\",\n            f\"{request.utterance}\",\n            f\"\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.fetched_context:\n            lines.append(\"## Fetched Context\")\n\n            # Add results from each source\n            for source, source_result in request.fetched_context.sources.items():\n                lines.append(f\"\\n### {source.value}\")\n                if source_result.status == \"success\":\n                    # Pretty-print the data\n                    lines.append(f\"```json\")\n                    lines.append(json.dumps(source_result.data, indent=2))\n                    lines.append(f\"```\")\n                else:\n                    lines.append(f\"Status: {source_result.status}\")\n                    if source_result.error:\n                        lines.append(f\"Error: {source_result.error}\")\n\n            # Add coverage info\n            if request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                lines.append(f\"\\n## Caveats\")\n                for caveat in request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                    lines.append(f\"- {caveat}\")\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\nPlease synthesize this into a structured result.\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# Global synthesize strand instance\n_synthesize_strand: Optional[SynthesizeStrand] = None\n\n\ndef get_synthesize_strand(prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> SynthesizeStrand:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global synthesize strand instance.\"\"\"\n    global _synthesize_strand\n    if _synthesize_strand is None:\n        _synthesize_strand = SynthesizeStrand(prompt_path=prompt_path)\n    return _synthesize_strand\n\n\nasync def synthesize_intent(request: SynthesizeRequest) -> SynthesizeResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to synthesize an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: SynthesizeRequest\n\n    Returns:\n        SynthesizeResult\n    \"\"\"\n    strand = get_synthesize_strand()\n    return await strand.synthesize(request)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":192,"oldLines":11,"newStart":192,"newLines":25,"lines":["             return result"," ","         except json.JSONDecodeError as e:","+            \"\"\"","+            Synthesize uses fallback result pattern (not corrective retry).","+","+            Reasoning: Synthesize runs AFTER expensive fetch operations.","+            Fetch data already obtained — should never be discarded.","+            Fallback result allows displaying raw data in degraded-state UX.","+            No retry needed (would re-run expensive fetch operations).","+","+            See docs/error-handling-standardization.md for pattern comparison.","+            \"\"\"","             logger.error(f\"Failed to parse synthesize response as JSON: {e}\")","-            # ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = content[0].get(\"text\", \"\")\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(f\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: dict,\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":12,"oldLines":6,"newStart":12,"newLines":33,"lines":[" - ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\n## Error Handling Patterns\n\nWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n\n### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n\nExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n\nSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n\n### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n\nExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n\nSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n\n### Documentation\nSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = content[0].get(\"text\", \"\")\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(f\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: dict,\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":50,"oldLines":8,"newStart":50,"newLines":18,"lines":[" "," "," class ParseLLMError(Exception):","-    \"\"\"Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\"\"\"","+    \"\"\"","+    Raised when LLM response parsing fails."," ","+    Attributes:","+        message: Human-readable error message","+        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)","+","+    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.","+    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include","+    response snippets in error events and logs.","+    \"\"\"","+","     def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):","         self.raw_response = raw_response","         super().__init__(message)"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

test_fence_no_newline_after_opening(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with no newline after opening marker.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        # Not standard markdown but some     # results in empty string because it splits on first \\n which is after the content\n        assert result == \"\"\n\n    def test_multiple_closing_fences(self):\n        \"\"\"Test content with multiple closing fence markers.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\", \"note\": \"end ```\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # rsplit removes the LAST ``` marker, keeping everything before it\n        # So we get the content with the proper closing fence, minus the extra ```\n        expected = f\"{content}\\n```\"\n        assert result == expected\n\n    def test_fence_in_middle_of_json(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence marker appearing within JSON structure.\"\"\"\n        # \"\\\"\", \"backslash\": \"\\\\\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"newline\"] == \"\\n\"\n        assert result[\"tab\"] == \"\\t\"\n        assert result[\"quote\"] == '\"'\n        assert result[\"backslash\"] == \"\\\\\"\n\n    def test_unicode_surrogate_pairs(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with characters requiring surrogate pairs.\"\"\"\n        # Using character outside BMP\n        content = '{\"symbol\": \"𝕿𝖍𝖎𝖘 𝖎𝖘 𝖆 𝖙𝖊𝖘𝖙\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert \"𝕿\" in result[\"symbol\"]\n\n    def test_mixed_line_endings(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with mixed line endings (\\\\r\\\\n vs \\\\n).\"\"\"\n        content = '{\\r\\n\"key\": \"value\"\\r\\n}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\r\\n{content}\\r\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"key\"] == \"value\"\n\n    def test_null_character_in_json_string(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON containing null character via escaped unicode.\"\"\"\n        # Using \\\\u0000 which is valid JSON escape for null character\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\\\\u0000end\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        # After JSON parsing, the null character should be in the string\n        assert result[\"key\"] == \"value\\x00end\"\n\n    def test_zero_width_joiner(self):\n        \"\"\"Test JSON with emoji zero-width joiner sequences.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"family\": \"👨‍👩‍👧‍👦\", \"skin_tone\": \"👋🏽\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        # These should be preserved\n        assert len(result[\"family\"]) > 0\n        assert len(result[\"skin_tone\"]) > 0\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Large Payload Edge Cases\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestLargePayloadEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test very large payloads to ensure no memory issues.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_very_large_payload_no_crash(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that very large payload doesn't cause crashes.\"\"\"\n        # Create a JSON payload ~1MB in size\n        large_data = {\n            \"items\": [\n                {\n                    \"id\": i,\n                    \"name\": f\"item-{i}\",\n                    \"description\": \"x\" * 100,  # 100 char description\n                    \"tags\": [f\"tag-{j}\" for j in range(10)],\n                    \"metadata\": {\n                        \"created\": 1234567890,\n                        \"updated\": 1234567890,\n                        \"active\": True\n                    }\n                }\n                for i in range(5000)  # ~5000 items\n            ]\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        # Should parse without error\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result[\"items\"]) == 5000\n        assert result[\"items\"][0][\"id\"] == 0\n\n    def test_large_nested_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test deeply nested JSON structure.\"\"\"\n        # Create a deeply nested structure\n        data = {\"level\": 0}\n        current = data\n        for i in range(100):  # 100 levels deep\n            current[\"nested\"] = {\"level\": i + 1}\n            current = current[\"nested\"]\n        current[\"value\"] = \"deep\"\n\n        large_json = json.dumps(data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert result[\"level\"] == 0\n\n    def test_large_array_with_unicode(self):\n        \"\"\"Test large array with many unicode entries.\"\"\"\n        data = {\n            \"unicode_items\": [\n                f\"item-{i}-你好-😊-مرحبا\"\n                for i in range(1000)\n            ]\n        }\n        large_json = json.dumps(data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        assert len(result[\"unicode_items\"]) == 1000\n        assert \"你好\" in result[\"unicode_items\"][0]\n\n    def test_large_payload_performance_reasonable(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that large payload parsing completes in reasonable time.\"\"\"\n        import time\n\n        # Create a 500KB payload\n        large_data = {\"data\": [\"x\" * 1000 for _ in range(500)]}\n        large_json = json.dumps(large_data)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{large_json}\\n```\"\n\n        start = time.perf_counter()\n        result = parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start\n\n        assert result is not None\n        # Should complete in less than 1 second even for large payload\n        assert elapsed < 1.0, f\"Large payload took {elapsed:.3f}s (too slow)\"\n\n    def test_malformed_large_payload_clear_error(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that malformed large payload gives clear error.\"\"\"\n        # Create a large malformed JSON\n        malformed = '{\"data\": [' + ', '.join(['\"x\"' * 100 for _ in range(1000)]) + '}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{malformed}\\n```\"\n\n        with pytest.raises(ParseLLMError) as exc_info:\n            parse_llm_response(fenced)\n        # Error message should include snippet\n        assert \"Failed to parse JSON\" in str(exc_info.value)\n        # Raw response should be available\n        assert exc_info.value.raw_response is not None\n\n\n# ============================================================================\n# Additional Fence Edge Cases\n# ============================================================================\n\nclass TestAdditionalFenceEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test additional edge cases around fence handling.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_fence_with_extra_whitespace_variations(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with various whitespace patterns.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # Whitespace before opening fence\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(f\"   ```json\\n{content}\\n```\")\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Whitespace after opening fence (but before newline)\n        fenced = f\"```json   \\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert \"key\" in result\n\n    def test_fence_case_sensitivity(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that fence language specifiers are case-sensitive.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # Lowercase (standard)\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Uppercase (non-standard but should still strip)\n        fenced = f\"```JSON\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n        # Mixed case\n        fenced = f\"```Json\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_fence_with_language_dialect(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with language dialect specifier.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n\n        # With dialect\n        fenced = f\"```json/python\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        assert \"key\" in result\n\n    def test_single_backtick_no_strip(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that single backtick is not treated as fence.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"`json\\n{content}\\n`\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip (only 1 backtick)\n        assert result.startswith(\"`json\")\n\n    def test_double_backtick_no_strip(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that double backtick is not treated as fence.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"``json\\n{content}\\n``\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should NOT strip (only 2 backticks)\n        assert result.startswith(\"``json\")\n\n    def test_fence_with_trailing_spaces_on_closing(self):\n        \"\"\"Test fence with spaces after closing marker.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\\n```   \"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # rsplit should handle trailing spaces\n        assert result == content\n\n    def test_multiple_fences_only_first_stripped(self):\n        \"\"\"Test content with fence-like opening that isn't actually a fence.\"\"\"\n        # This tests a case where content contains what looks like a fence\n        content = '{\"text\": \"This is not a fence: ```json\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```\\n{content}\\n```\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should strip the outer fences but preserve inner one\n        assert \"```json\" in result\n\n    def test_incomplete_fence_only_opening(self):\n        \"\"\"Test string with only opening fence, no closing.\"\"\"\n        content = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        fenced = f\"```json\\n{content}\"\n        result = strip_markdown_fences(fenced)\n        # Should strip opening fence and return rest\n        assert
